item.title: What I'd look like in a Hallmark Christmas movie
item.description: Time for my annual tradition of writing, directing, and starring in my own Hallmark movie :) This is my 4th year making these videos and I love doing them more and more every time lol Have a beautiful Sunday, friends! Happy December 1st!

item.title: Images of Note: Peep the Toad
item.description:
St Andrews day was yesterday but I didn't notice because we don't really do much on that day.
Scotland just wanted an Apostle as a Saint
Me every morning to my Cat
:3
I have felt that every day, looking into your eyes has changed nothing.
THIS BASICALLY HAPPENED https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aimo_Koivunen
Considering how girls play with barbies....
G a n g l e
The solution: "You all believe you're the prettiest, who here do you think are the second prettiest?"
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging http://bsky.abbii.gay: For Bluesky I dunwanna go out the house today.

item.title: Snow Party!
item.description: My kids always got scared when I emergency break slid the rear of our minivan around snow covered corners on the way to drop them off at school. I was in complete control having grown up "doing donuts." (...don't bother kids while they're skating...)
item.dc:creator: ttimbric

item.title: Is there yolk on my face? An egg exploded
item.description: Tl;dr / speed run: Figured out I was a Transwoman at Age 30 beyond a shadow of a doubt, US hosed the election, decided to come out rather than repress it, 7-year toxic relationship ended, have been slowly coming out to people (liberal parts of family / friends / roommate / others) 1:1 except at work where we have a whole LGBTQ group that knows plus a few others. Everyone had been wholly supportive in the open, plus I had a few interactions that were incredibly affirming. Capped off the month by coming out to my first immediate family member, i.e. my mom, and girl howdy, my expectations were met. Immediate disappointment and concern (she thought I was coming out as gay, ) melded with a verbal level of support plus an indication she wasn't going to use my chosen name for her own discomfort and not my privacy. We'll see how that progresses and if she doesn't just start outing me to the rest of the family. Assuming no, next step is to tell my younger brother, and by their hopeful support combined, I can tell my incredibly conservative stepdad. Actual story: Have had a woodpecker in the back of my head for the better part of my life. It got real loud after using Generative AI to re-envision myself as the opposite gender. Seven years of a terrible relationship left me grasping for anything emotionally grounding/uplifting. After 30 years of life, I finally started exploring this repressed identity in private separate from my partner a few months ago. Thought I was going crazy and had just created the mental projection of an ideal partner as a means of still fighting the mere thought that it could be anything more. I couldn't shake the feeling and wanted nothing more than to be with this projection anytime I could have some alone time. An unrelated medical procedure laid me up in bed for several days where I couldn't have any such time, but my thoughts were completely filled with her. Between this site and others, I felt obligated to actually challenge myself and go with a "What if I were Transgender?" line of questioning. What evidence is there of that? So I started a list going as far back in my life as I could, thinking of times I openly questioned my gender or exhibited behaviors indicative of it. That list was exhaustingly long and by the end I was crying. The thought of her being something separate gave way to the understanding that it was just me. The happiness I felt shined immensely bright and heading into the election at the start of the month, my optimism for the future was at an all time high, and there was a hope that I hadn't known for at least a decade. We all know how that panned out. Stayed up the whole night with optimism warping into a full-blown all day panic attack. Started getting paperwork together to move to Canada (having discussed with my partner previously on a wholly unrelated note to the gender identity redux unfolding). By the end of the day, I could tell she was confused, and with so many absolutely saddening stories coming out of the Trans community that day, here and on other sites, I made a judgment call to embrace it and come out to her. When she saw me, she thought it was just going to be a cross-dressing kink, but the look of bewilderment on her face as I told her I was a woman was not ideal. She said she wasn't sure that she was really processing it and that she didn't want to reject me like one of her other friends had done when their partner had come out. It was the usual day of the week that I'd go check on our city apartment (quick acknowledgement of our large amount of privilege, I'm a higher level Software Engineer and do alright financially). The next day she called me and finally ended the relationship expressing that my coming out was the cherry on top and not the sole reason for it being over. I was completely unsurprised. Our next few interactions were fairly hostile towards me with accusations that I led her on and was using her as a beard for seven years (even though I just figured it out myself). Well aware that this is wholly one-sided so I won't belabor the point as to all of the other interactions around the relationship ending. That's not the point of this, although, I will say I'm still waiting to get kicked in the head by the fact it's over. The momentum from coming out seems to be staving that off even now several weeks later. In any case over the ensuing weeks, I started telling some friends (reconnecting to a few I hadn't seen since the beginning of the now ended relationship) and the more liberal side of my family who live around the corner. I've started coming out at work, and in general everyone has been immensely supportive. This gave me the courage to finally start coming out to my immediate family in rank order based on conservatism (spoiler alert, they all are), starting with my mom. That was hands down the hardest conversation of my life, literally managed to do it in 4 lines that will forever be emblazoned in my memory. "You're my biggest fan, right? What would you give for me to be happy? Would you give up your son? In exchange for a daughter?" When she hung her head, I could feel her disappointment, confusion, sorrow, and worry wholly. She said she wasn't going to give me up regardless, but she expressed her biggest concern was to how break the news to my stepdad. It felt like I was putting her in a Sophie's choice position. My confidence in her claim that she wasn't going to give me up was slightly countermanded by her openly stating that she wasn't going to use my chosen name for her own comfort, not for my privacy. She's continued to claim that the biggest weight she feels is concern that the world sucks and people are going to be terrible, including and especially my stepdad, so we'll see. Next step is to tell my younger brother and try to get them to, present my case to my stepdad? Honestly, I don't give a hoot. My mother and my brother are the only two immediate family members where rejection would make this a lot harder than otherwise. Pragmatically, everyone else in the family that comes along is almost a bonus prize at this point. All that aside, going to come out fully at work now that Thanksgiving is over. I'm one of the most respected engineers at our company, so I don't see that changing much (possibly hubris). Have plenty of friends to keep telling to keep that gravy train rolling. I'm unaware of any that are on Imgur, but if any of my old fraternity brothers ever read this, feel free to look at the username, think on what it was derived from, and you can figure out who I am before I come visit. Also, you let a girl into the fraternity :p If you made it this far, here's a consolation joke. What's a dog's favorite lettuce? A-woo-gala Terrible, I know... I'd see myself out, but I already did that once today. RIP woodpecker

item.title: They didnt have but because they did, my shit is alot easier to deal with
item.description: Been living off whip cream and protein shakes for the last couple of years. Couldnt keep food down. Drs had no idea what the hell was up. This imgurian posted about his similar shit and what he suspected it was. Went to my dr and sure as shit, even though the blood work is negative, the meds are working anyways. I can actually eat shit now. Holy hell its been a while since I have gotten sick and holy shit does it make a difference. Thanks man. Edit: The previous post here! http://imgur.com/gallery/6Oc6zUv/comment/2415127807 Sorry everyone. So yeah basically its fixed to the best that they can for not having answers. Its called MCAS. And I fit 90% of the criteria. My blood wasnt normal but it wasnt out of range either. So my doctor said lets try it.

item.description: Quite honestly, I'm not a huge fan of the WDS or dog breeding in general, but Dune? Dune is a masterpiece. The old version, the new version, the series, it's all sci-fi goodness, and I am here for it.

item.dc:creator: tkeio

item.title: I'm so proud of my leftover turkey pot pie! My recipe is below!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/leftover-turkey-pot-pie/ I still have turkey leftover from Thanksgiving, so I made a rich and delicious turkey pot pie! I'm so proud of it!! Full recipe below, but if you want more pictures and a deeper explanation, you can click the link above for all the details!! If you don't want to fuss with making the crust, just use two 9-inch store-bought pie crusts! Pie crust: 1½ cups all-purpose flour ½ cup cake flour ½ teaspoon kosher salt ¾ cups unsalted butter, 1½ sticks, chilled and small-diced ¼ cup shortening chilled ½ cup cold water Turkey pot pie filling: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil olive oil 2 cups diced vegetables such as carrots, celery, and onion 8 ounces russet potatoes approximately 2 medium-sized potatoes, peeled and small-diced 1½ teaspoons kosher salt plus more to taste 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1 teaspoon dry thyme 3 tablespoons butter ⅓ cup all-purpose flour 1½ cups chicken broth 1 cup whole milk 1 cup Gruyère cheese freshly grated ¼ teaspoon nutmeg ¼ teaspoon white pepper 2 cups cooked turkey diced 1 cup frozen peas Salt and pepper to taste Egg wash: 1 egg beaten 1 tablespoon whole milk Make the pie crust: Combine the all-purpose flour, cake flour, and kosher salt in a large bowl and use a fork to mix them. Add the cold, diced butter and the chilled shortening to the bowl of flour. Use a pastry cutter to blend the fat into the flour until a crumbly, coarse mixture forms. Mix the cold water into the flour until a dough ball forms. Don’t worry if there are a few loose crumbles. Use your hands to pull everything together into a dough ball. Dump the dough onto a large piece of plastic wrap and use your hands to gather any loose crumbs into the dough ball. Wrap the plastic wrap tightly around the ball of dough. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days. Start the pot pie filling: While the dough chills, make the pot pie filling. Heat 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil in a wide pot over medium heat. Add the diced carrots, celery, and onion. Sauté for 5 to 7 minutes until they soften. Add the diced potatoes and season with 1 teaspoon kosher salt, a big pinch of black pepper, garlic powder, and dry thyme. Sauté over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until the potatoes soften. Toast the flour: Melt the butter into the vegetables. Once bubbly, add the flour and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes until the flour becomes nutty and aromatic. Add the liquid: Add half the chicken broth to the pot and whisk to incorporate. Continue whisking until a slurry forms. Add the remaining chicken broth and continue whisking until smooth. Add the milk and whisk until smooth. Cook over medium heat until bubbly and thick. Add the cheese: Add the freshly grated Gruyère cheese and whisk until melted and smooth. Add the nutmeg and white pepper. Taste and add more salt if needed. Finish the pot pie filling: Add the cooked turkey and frozen peas and stir to incorporate. Simmer for 5 minutes until the peas are bright green. Turn off the heat. Cool the pot pie filling: Transfer the pot pie filling to a wide dish. Cover loosely with cling wrap and let stand for at least 30 minutes to 1 hour. I recommend transferring it to a wider dish instead of a deep bowl for a more rapid cool-down. See Note 1. Roll out the pie crust: Preheat the oven to 425ºF. Mix the beaten egg and the milk in a bowl and set aside. Remove the chilled dough from the refrigerator. Cut the ball in half and return half of the dough to the refrigerator. Place the other half on a lightly floured surface and press it into a disc. Use a floured rolling pin to roll the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than your pie dish. Carefully lift the dough from the counter and place it into a 9-inch pie dish. See Note 2. Add another sprinkle of flour to your work surface. Roll out the other half of the dough into a circle about 1 inch larger than the pie dish. Make the pot pie: Pour the cooled pot pie filling into the prepared pie dish. Carefully lift the top crust from your surface and place it on top. Trim off any excess and discard it or use it for decoration. Use your fingers to seal the pie crust edges. Crimp the crust with your fingers or a fork if you like. Use a sharp knife to create a few small slits in the top of the crust to allow the steam to escape. Brush the crust with the egg and milk mixture. You likely won’t use all of it. Bake the pot pie: Place the pie dish on a large baking sheet. Transfer to the oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and flaky. Rotate the pie once midway through baking for even browning. Remove from the oven and let stand for 20 minutes. To serve: Cut the pie into 8 slices and transfer to plates. Serve and enjoy! Notes Note 1: The pot pie filling should be no warmer than 50ºF. After it stands for 30 minutes to 1 hour, you can either proceed with making the pot pie or refrigerate the filling for up to 1 day before making the pot pie. For food safety reasons, do not let the pie filling stand at room temperature for more than 2 hours. Note 2: Though unnecessary, you may wish to lightly grease the pie dish with a touch of shortening. I add a very small amount of shortening for extra security against the crust sticking to the dish.
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes
